title: Eduversal Classroom Walkthrough — Observation Guide
description: A structured guide for short, unannounced classroom visits (typically 5–10 minutes). Walkthroughs are formative in nature and carry no score. Observers record narrative comments and a presence indicator for each focus area. Findings are shared with the teacher as developmental feedback — not used in annual appraisal calculations.

response_options.label: Not Observed
response_options.description: Not seen during this visit — may be due to lesson phase, not absence of practice

foci.title: Learning Intentions
foci.guiding_question: Are students aware of what they are learning and why?
foci.description: The observer notes whether the lesson's purpose is made explicit to students — through a stated objective, a success criterion displayed or verbalised, or a framing that anchors student activity to a clear goal. Awareness can be inferred by asking a student.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe how (or whether) learning intentions were communicated. Note any success criteria or purpose statements visible or spoken.
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with Danielson FfT Component 1c (Setting Instructional Outcomes) and Marzano's Design Question 1 (What will students learn?).

foci.title: Student Engagement
foci.guiding_question: Are students actively engaged in the learning activity?
foci.description: The observer notes the proportion and nature of student engagement at the moment of visit — whether students are on task, the quality of engagement (passive listening, active processing, collaborative), and any signs of disengagement.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe what students were doing. Estimate the proportion on-task. Note whether engagement is passive, active, or collaborative.
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with Marzano's Design Question 6 (Engaging students) and Danielson FfT Component 3c (Engaging Students in Learning).

foci.title: Classroom Climate
foci.guiding_question: Is the environment orderly, respectful, and conducive to learning?
foci.description: The observer notes the physical and relational environment: student behaviour, the teacher's demeanour and interactions, and whether the atmosphere supports learning. Includes arrangement of space, noise level appropriate to the activity, and visible respect between teacher and students.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe the classroom atmosphere. Note the quality of teacher–student interactions and any indicators of a positive or challenging climate.
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with Danielson FfT Component 2a (Creating an Environment of Respect and Rapport) and 2b (Establishing a Culture for Learning).

foci.title: Instructional Strategy
foci.guiding_question: Is the teaching approach appropriate for the stated objective?
foci.description: The observer notes whether the instructional method in use (direct instruction, guided practice, group work, discussion, hands-on activity, etc.) is a purposeful match for the lesson's learning goal and student need. Variety across visits is also noted over time.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe the teaching strategy observed. Note whether it appears aligned to the lesson objective and appropriate for the learners.
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with Danielson FfT Component 1e (Designing Coherent Instruction) and Marzano's Design Question 5 (What strategies will I use?).

foci.title: Formative Monitoring
foci.guiding_question: Is the teacher checking understanding and adjusting accordingly?
foci.description: The observer notes whether the teacher is actively gathering evidence of student understanding — through questioning, circulating, scanning student work, or using checks — and whether there is any visible responsiveness to that evidence within the lesson.
foci.prompt_for_observer: Describe how the teacher monitored student understanding. Note any visible adjustments made in response to student responses or work.
foci.theory_basis: Aligned with Danielson FfT Component 3d (Using Assessment in Instruction) and Marzano's Design Question 8 (What will I do to help students track and reflect on their learning?).

narrative_fields.label: Key Observation
narrative_fields.description: A brief, specific observation about what was most notable during this visit — a strength, a pattern, or something worth discussing.

narrative_fields.label: Suggested Focus
narrative_fields.description: One specific, actionable suggestion or professional learning focus for the teacher to consider before the next visit. Not prescriptive — framed as an invitation to reflect.

metadata_fields.label: Lesson Phase at Time of Visit
